sections.name: open
sections.notes: Sparse density — harmony re-strikes at bar boundaries only. Should sound like a pad player holding long notes with a fresh attack each bar. NOT a wall of whole notes.

sections.name: stir
sections.notes: Medium density — harmony hits beats 1 and 3, with occasional ghost notes. Chord movement is faster so the articulation pattern is clearly audible across chord changes.

sections.name: push
sections.notes: Full density — harmony plays quarter notes with accent on beats 1 and 3, occasional breath rest on beat 4. The groove makes the chord changes swing. This is the Africa test.

sections.name: motif_driven
sections.notes: Harmony rhythm driven by the motif rhythm — [1.0, 0.5, 0.5, 1.0, 1.0]. The chord articulates on exactly those durations, tiled across the section. The motif shape is audible in the harmony layer.

sections.name: groove_push
sections.notes: Backbeat groove applied to harmony — harmony hits fall on the push pattern. Medium density, but the groove gives it rhythmic personality rather than mechanical grid.

sections.name: return
sections.notes: Back to sparse — but now the ear has heard the full range. Same chord as the opening but feels earned. Harmony re-strikes per bar, long sustains, fade out.
